summary: Tier 1: strip '^Sacred Texts ... Previous Next' nav prefix (the ~30% win) + re-chunk/re-embed
description:
Tier 1 / the 80/20: strip the leading site-nav prefix from chunk bodies.

Pattern (collection word varies — Classics / Buddhism / Zoroastrianism / Egypt / Esoteric):
    ^Sacred Texts\b.*?\bPrevious\s+Next\b\s*      (non-greedy)
971 local chunks (30%) match. Add it to pre_strip_patterns in every chunking
config whose text has nav-prefixed chunks. By tradition (local counts):
neoplatonism=716 (plotinus-select-works-index — page-as-chunk, currently NO
pre_strip), taoism=137, egyptian=68, zoroastrianism=19, christian_mysticism=14,
greek_mystery=10, jewish_mysticism=4, hermeticism=2, buddhism=1 (diamond-sutra).
Enumerate the exact text_ids by re-running the corroboration scan.

Mechanism notes:
- pre_strip is applied PER-PAGE for page-as-chunk/multi sources and whole-file
  for single sources; both anchor "^", so each page's/file's leading nav is
  removed. The round-trip test mirrors this exactly (tests/test_chunking.py
  _load_raw_text), so it will stay valid.
- Re-chunk affected texts, then refresh local guru.db: bootstrap_chunks (upsert
  node labels) + cleanup_stale_chunk_nodes --apply (if chunk counts shift) +
  embed_corpus --tradition X (INSERT OR REPLACE refreshes vectors) — the exact
  sequence used in todo:73c83a22.

Done contract:
- A scan reports 0 chunks whose body starts with the nav prefix.
- tests/test_chunking.py round-trip stays green.
- Local guru.db has no stale chunk_ids for affected texts; embeddings refreshed.
- Regression test: assert no chunk body matches ^Sacred Texts\b.*Previous\s+Next.
